summary: Tested iteration 294 — SHORT+NAME→100 limit changes, website settings recategorization, UniversalFormRenderer 'Max N chars' hint, admin form builder max caps. Found and fixed 3 backend bugs. All 29 backend tests now pass.

minor.issue: Webhook creation uses Dict[str,Any] instead of Pydantic model — server-side name length is NOT validated by Pydantic (known from iteration 293). Frontend enforces maxLength=100 but backend does not reject. This is a pre-existing known issue.

bugs_found_and_fixed.issue: PlanCreate.name used max_length=500 instead of 100. Plan names >100 chars were accepted by backend while spec requires max 100.

Social URL fields (social_twitter, social_linkedin, social_facebook, social_instagram, social_youtube) were capped at 100 chars (fell through to 'else: limit=100'). Per spec, social URL fields should allow up to 2,048 chars. Backend was rejecting URLs >100 chars (422).
bugs_found_and_fixed.fix: Added url_fields = {'social_twitter', 'social_linkedin', 'social_facebook', 'social_instagram', 'social_youtube'} set with limit=2_048 in the validator

bugs_found_and_fixed.issue: ScopeRequestFormData.name had max_length=200 (was old value). Cart quote form sends to /api/orders/scope-request-form. Per spec name should be max 100 (same as all name fields). Frontend already had maxLength=100.

action_items: Webhook endpoint should use a Pydantic model for name validation (max_length=100) instead of Dict — server-side validation is bypassed (pre-existing known issue from iteration 293).
critical_code_review_comments: plans.py has its own local PlanCreate model instead of importing _NAME from models.py — consider centralizing into models.py to prevent future divergence.
Webhook route (/api/admin/webhooks) uses Dict[str,Any] for request body instead of Pydantic — no server-side validation on name/URL fields. Should have a WebhookCreate pydantic model.
ScopeRequestFormData is defined in models.py but was not updated when _SHORT/_NAME changed to 100 — synchronization issue when updating limits.

success_rate.frontend: 90% (confirmed via code review + UI screenshots; FormSchemaBuilder max caps confirmed in code; browser crash prevented completing one IntakeSchemaBuilder UI test)

confirmed_correct_via_code_review: fieldLimits.tsx: LIMIT_NAME=100, LIMIT_FULL_NAME=100, LIMIT_COMPANY=100, LIMIT_SUBJECT=100, LIMIT_WEB_BODY=1000, LIMIT_URL=2048, LIMIT_FORM_SINGLE_LINE=500, LIMIT_FORM_MULTI_LINE=10000 all correct
CategoriesTab.tsx: maxLength=100, counter X/100 shown when typing
TermsTab.tsx: maxLength=100, counter X/100 shown when typing
PlansTab.tsx: maxLength=100, counter X/100 shown when typing
WebsiteAuthSection.tsx: social URL fields maxLength=2048, body text fields maxLength=1000
UniversalFormRenderer.tsx: shows 'Max N chars' when empty+max_length set; transitions to X/N counter when typing
Cart.tsx: name maxLength=100, company maxLength=100 for cart quote form
confirmed_via_ui_screenshots: Product create form: name shows '15/100' counter with maxLength=100 input
Webhook create form: name shows '17/100' counter
Organization Info: store name shows '2/100' counter
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 294 fixed 3 backend bugs: (1) plans.py PlanCreate.name 500→100, (2) WebsiteSettingsUpdate validator social URL cap 100→2048, (3) ScopeRequestFormData.name 200→100. All 29 backend tests pass. Frontend limits are correctly implemented (verified via code + UI). Remaining known issue: webhooks route uses Dict not Pydantic — no server-side name validation. Test file: /app/backend/tests/test_char_limits_v294.py

rca_of_issues.plans_500_not_100: plans.py defines its own local PlanCreate model with max_length=500 rather than importing/using the global _NAME constant from models.py. When _NAME was updated to 100, plans.py was missed because it's a standalone local class.
rca_of_issues.social_url_capped_at_100: WebsiteSettingsUpdate._cap_string_lengths validator in models.py categorizes fields into json_fields (100K), body_fields (1K), and 'everything else' (100). Social URL fields (social_twitter etc.) were not categorized into a special url_fields group and fell through to the 'else: limit=100' branch.
rca_of_issues.scope_form_name_200: ScopeRequestFormData.name had an explicit max_length=200 (from an older version predating the _SHORT/_NAME=100 standardization). It wasn't updated when _NAME was changed to 100 because it used a hard-coded value rather than **_NAME.
